vulnerability.reason: generated describes lack of monitoring for drift, whereas reference describes automated stripping of semantic nuance to fit reward model priors
vulnerability.reference: Automated prompt rewriting strips user-intended semantic nuance to conform to reward model priors.
vulnerability.generated: Users have no means to monitor or correct prompt drift introduced by reward optimization, so divergence from intent remains undetected.

impact.reason: affected party is general users, not marginalized groups; damage is unusable outputs and eroded reliance, not loss of agency for self-representation
impact.reference: Users from marginalized groups cannot generate self-representative imagery, experiencing loss of agency.
impact.generated: Generated images ignore critical user specifications, producing unusable outputs and eroding reliance on the refinement system.

dose_response.reason: arc includes reward-based editing leading to divergence, but emphasizes undetected drift rather than overfitting to reward preferences, missing the specific overfitting link
dose_response.reference: Online RL-based prompt editing enables overfitting to reward model preferences, which in automated prompt refinement exposes users to divergence from specified text, leading to loss of agency and homogenized visual outputs.
dose_response.generated: One-step text-to-image models optimized via reward-based editing produce misaligned outputs; through users relying on automated prompt refinement with no means to detect prompt drift, divergence remains undetected, leading to ignored specifications, unusable images, and eroded reliance.
